Mark SIGGRAPH Asia 2026 on your calendar! The Call for submission portal is now up!

1 – 4 December 2026

KLCC, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

Conference  1 – 4 December 2026
Exhibition 2 – 4 December 2026
KLCC, Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia

 

The 19th ACM SIGGRAPH Conference and Exhibition on Computer Graphics and Interactive Techniques in Asia, and the first edition ever in Malaysia!

SIGGRAPH ASIA 2026

WEAVING
THE FUTURE
Submissions
Submissions
Submit your latest innovations & be a presenter at SIGGRAPH Asia in Kuala Lumpur
Read More
Exhibition
Exhibition
Secure your booth & presence at the largest CG event in Asia
Read More
Sponsorship
Sponsorship
Stand out amongst all the brands by getting a customised sponsorship package
Read More
Malaysia
Malaysia
Explore Malaysia while you are here at SIGGRAPH Asia
Read More

ABOUT SIGGRAPH ASIA 2026

SIGGRAPH Asia 2026 will mark a new milestone as it takes place in Kuala Lumpur for the first time, expanding the conference’s footprint and welcoming fresh perspectives. This edition promises to enrich our community by fostering new connections and enhancing the experience for all attendees. With strong support from both local and international partners, we are confident that SIGGRAPH Asia 2026 will be a memorable and successful event. I look forward to welcoming you to Kuala Lumpur in December 2026!
READ MORE

Frank Guan

SIGGRAPH Asia 2026 Conference Chair,
Singapore Institute of Technology, Singapore

of SIGGRAPH Asia

Testimonials

“SIGGRAPH Asia was a remarkable experience that truly dives into each professional's and researcher's passions to bring art and technology together harmoniously.”

Brent Simon

Validation Engineer, NVIDIA

“It was a mind-blowing fusion of cutting-edge tech and cultural creativity, from next-gen animation to interactive installations. It was incredibly well-orchestrated, with each surprise around the corner proving why this conference remains a must-attend event for anyone interested in computer graphics, art and interactive techniques.”

Chen Yang

Doctoral Student, Computer Graphics Lab, ETHZ

“SIGGRAPH Asia proved to be an exceptionally inspiring event! Each talk and exhibit not only left me motivated to improve my own workflow, but also sparked my intrigue about future technologies that I wasn’t yet aware of.”

Danielle Gabb

3D Artist, Intr Studio

“SIGGRAPH Asia is the place to learn, network and be inspired about everything in computer graphics industry. You will also make lifelong friendships here and every year traveling to different country gives attendees the unique cultural experience beyond industry talks and exhibitions.”

Shuyi Yang

Carleton University

Keynote Speakers

READ MORE KEYNOTE SESSIONS
Jun Murai received his Ph.D. in Computer Science, Keio University in 1987, majoring in Computer Science, the Internet, and Computer Communication. Jun Murai is the founder of JUNET (the first network in Japan connecting multiple universities) and WIDE Project (a Japanese Internet research consortium). He has long been engaged in research related to Internet technology platforms and is known as ‘The Father of the Internet in Japan’ and in international circles as the Internet Samurai.

Jun Murai

Distinguished Professor, Keio University Founder, WIDE Project
Jun Murai received his Ph.D. in Computer Science, Keio University in 1987, majoring in Computer Science, the Internet, and Computer Communication. Jun Murai is the founder of JUNET (the first network in Japan connecting multiple universities) and WIDE Project (a Japanese Internet research consortium). He has long been engaged in research related to Internet technology platforms and is known as ‘The Father of the Internet in Japan’ and in international circles as the Internet Samurai.

Jun Murai

Distinguished Professor, Keio University Founder, WIDE Project
